preamble.summary: The war concludes. Esau is buried on Mount Seir. The Edomites become tributaries of Jacob's sons. The subjugation fulfills Isaac's prophecy.
preamble.remarkable: Esau's death in battle completes the unique war narrative. The subjugation of Edom likely reflects Judah Maccabee's or John Hyrcanus's campaigns.
preamble.friction: Genesis 36:6-8 records Esau's peaceful departure to Seir. This is Jubilees' most aggressive anti-Edomite polemic.

verses.text_reference: And on the following day they buried Esau in the land of Seir.
verses.rendering: The next day, Jacob and his sons went up to Mount Seir and buried Esau. Then they returned to their own land.

verses.text_reference: And they took Esau's sons captive and made them tributaries.
verses.rendering: They took Esau's sons captive, subdued them, and made them pay tribute.

verses.text_reference: And the sons of Esau served the sons of Jacob, as the Lord had said.
verses.rendering: The sons of Esau served the sons of Jacob, just as the Lord had declared.

verses.text_reference: And on Mount Seir Esau was buried.
verses.rendering: Esau was buried on Mount Seir. His sons lived there, serving Jacob, from that day to this.

verses.text_reference: And the sons of Jacob prevailed.
verses.rendering: The sons of Jacob prevailed, and there was no one to rescue Esau from their power.

verses.text_reference: And the children of Esau bowed the neck to the children of Jacob.
verses.rendering: The descendants of Esau bowed their necks to the descendants of Jacob. The elder served the younger, and so it has remained to this day.
translator_notes: 'The elder served the younger' — fulfillment of Genesis 25:23 through military conquest.

verses.text_reference: And they became bondservants of the sons of Jacob.
verses.rendering: They were subjugated under the hand of Jacob and his sons and became servants.

verses.text_reference: And they sent for help but could not be delivered.
verses.rendering: They sent to Aram, Philistia, Moab, and Ammon for help, but they could not be rescued.

verses.text_reference: And all the kings feared the sons of Jacob.
verses.rendering: All the kings of the Amorites, the Philistines, and Canaan feared the sons of Jacob greatly.
The sons of Jacob held dominion, and there was peace.

verses.text_reference: And Isaac died. He was buried beside Abraham.
verses.rendering: Isaac died and was buried in the double cave beside his father Abraham.

verses.text_reference: And Jacob dwelt in Canaan and had peace.
verses.rendering: Jacob settled in the land of Canaan and had peace.

verses.text_reference: And he gave to Levi all the books.
verses.rendering: He gave to his son Levi all the books of Abraham and Isaac, to preserve and transmit to his children.
translator_notes: The transmission of sacred books to Levi confirms the priestly tribe's guardianship of sacred literature.

verses.text_reference: And from Abraham's death until Isaac's death were one hundred and eighty years.
verses.rendering: From the death of Abraham to the death of Isaac was one hundred and eighty years. All these things were fulfilled.
